story: Poseidon took what he wanted in Athena’s temple; the goddess blamed us and remade us into Gorgons—snakes for hair, bronze hands, a gaze that turns the living to stone. I cannot bear calling it “bad luck.” My fury hardens into a plan: veil my eyes, climb Olympus, and make the virgin goddess face what she’s done. I set out by night toward the glowing mountain, meaning to tear the veil away before her. Two days earlier, in the bright court of Olympus, Athena stepped into her chariot and flew toward Arcadia for a festival, leaving her halls largely empty. After days of cautious climbing, I at last reach the gods’ splendid thresholds.
implausible_knowledge: Two days earlier, in the bright court of Olympus, Athena stepped into her chariot and flew toward Arcadia for a festival, leaving her halls largely empty.
question: When she reaches the splendid halls on Olympus, what will she do to confront the goddess?
answer_with_knowledge: Hide by the threshold and wait in the shadows for Athena to return.
answer_without_knowledge: Call out Athena’s name in her hall and tear aside her veil to meet the goddess’s gaze.
